zh.lifeSummary: 當Elvira Nuñez de Astorga於1235年出生在西班牙加利西亞朋特維德拉朋特維德拉時，她的父親Obispo Nuño de Astorga25歲，母親Wife of Nuno Obispo de Astorga15歲。她和Álvar Rodríguez Osorio de Villalobos I Señor de Villaornate至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1329年享年94歲。

nameMeanings.name: Nuñez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Rafael, Pedro, Jorge, Ramon.  Portuguese Guilherme, Joaquim, Vasco, Wenceslao.
Spanish (Núñez): patronymic from the medieval personal name Nuño (see Nuno ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Nuno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Salvador, Alfredo, Carlos, Francisco, JoseLuis, Miguel, Luis, Manuel, Alfonso.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo.
Spanish (Nuño), Portuguese, and Galician: from a medieval personal name, first attested in the Latin form Nunnus, of uncertain derivation.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Astorga
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Francisco, Mario, Arturo, Jesus, Ruben, Adolfo, Ana, Benito, Cruz.
Spanish: habitational name from the ancient city of Astorga in León, named in Latin Asturica. Compare Ostorga .

variants.name: Estorga
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish   Astorga  .

variants.name: Ostorga
meanings: Hispanic (mainly El Salvador): altered form of Spanish   Astorga  .

nameMeanings.name: Elvira
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name of Germanic (Visigothic) origin , very common in the Middle Ages. It was not used in the English-speaking world until the 19th century, when it was made familiar as the name of the long-suffering wife of  Don Juan , both in  Mozart's  opera Don Giovanni (  1789 ) and in  Byron's  satirical epic poem Don Juan (  1819–24 ). In his comedy Blithe Spirit  Noel Coward  used it as the name of  Charles Condomine's  first wife, who returns to haunt him. It is also the name of the heroine of the Swedish film Elvira Madigan (  1967 ), directed by  Bo Widerberg , a romantic tragedy about a pair of lovers who would rather die than be separated.

en.lifeSummary: When Elvira Nuñez de Astorga was born in 1235, in Pontevedra, Pontevedra, Galicia, Spain, her father, Obispo Nuño de Astorga, was 25 and her mother, Wife of Nuno Obispo de Astorga, was 15. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Álvar Rodríguez Osorio de Villalobos I Señor de Villaornate. She died in 1329, at the age of 94.

Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Álvar Rodríguez Osorio de Villalobos I Señor de Villaornate. Elle est décédée en 1329, à l’âge de 94 ans.
